hooks.text: Your credit card has a high limit. It still cannot pay your supplier past 12,000 dollars. The number on the card is not the real ceiling.
There is an exact dollar amount where paying a China supplier by card just stops working. It is not your credit limit. It is far lower.
Card payments to Chinese suppliers on Alibaba cap at 12,000 dollars per order. One dollar over and the card is dead. That number is not random.
A business owner builds the order, picks the supplier, taps the card. It works at 11,000 dollars. The same card fails at 13,000. Same factory.
Scale your order past 12,000 dollars on a card and the payment fails at checkout. The supplier waits, the slot slips, and the deal can quietly die.
